---
title: ''
institute: ''
author: ''
date: 2025-10-21
categories:
- gpu
- llm
- attention
- memory
- systems
description: llmsys
image: ''
background: Below is a compact executive brief. Per your reporting format, the evidence
  table comes first in the main body, followed by math digest, the replicable mini‑audit,
  synthesis/taxonomy, and a repro kit.
format: html
---

Below is a compact executive brief. Per your **reporting format**, the **evidence table** comes first in the main body, followed by math digest, the replicable mini‑audit, synthesis/taxonomy, and a repro kit.


## 0) Executive brief (≤1 page)

**What’s solid (2023→2025):**

* **Outliers are real and structured.** Channel‑wise activation outliers (persistent high‑variance channels) hamper A‑quant; token‑wise “massive activations” are rarer but drive W/A/KV low‑bit failures. Mitigations with **migration** (SmoothQuant), **channel re‑centering/scaling** (OS+), and **orthogonal rotations** (QuaRot/SpinQuant/DFRot) consistently reduce error and enable **W4A4** on many LLMs; K/V behave differently and benefit from **asymmetric schemes** (K per‑channel, V per‑token) at **2‑bit** (KIVI). ([arXiv][1])
* **Attention is sparse and skewed.** Heads show **redundancy** (many are prunable/shareable) and **sink** bias (BOS/initial tokens collect attention mass), which both affect long‑context stability and KV policies (StreamingLLM). ([arXiv][2])
* **Similarity reveals structure.** Across layers/heads, Q/K/V projections have low effective rank and high inter‑head similarity in places; **CKA/PWCCA** reliably capture cross‑layer/head redundancy for clustering/pruning diagnostics. ([arXiv][3])
* **KV cache is the serving bottleneck.** Quantization and redundancy‑aware dropping/compression (e.g., H2O heavy‑hitters; LeanKV unified quant+prune) materially cut memory/latency with minor accuracy loss; **K and V need different treatment**. ([NeurIPS Proceedings][4])
* **Kernel reality matters.** Practical wins depend on **SDPA/FlashAttention** for dense and **xFormers BlockSparse** (or Triton) for sparse/rotated paths; mis‑matched kernels can negate algorithmic gains. ([PyTorch Documentation][5])

**What’s unsettled (and where papers disagree):**

* **Rotations: random vs learned.** QuaRot shows random (Hadamard/orthogonal) rotations suffice for 4‑bit end‑to‑end on some families; SpinQuant/DFRot find **rotation choice is crucial**, with **learned** rotations (Cayley) or refined criteria outperforming random by large margins on hard models (e.g., Llama‑3‑8B). Likely causes: calibration set composition, token‑wise outliers (“massive activations”), and layernorm variants. ([NeurIPS Proceedings][6])
* **Attention sinks: artifact or useful feature?** StreamingLLM **uses** sinks to stabilize long‑context; newer analyses argue sinks are tied to softmax normalization and may be **non‑informative “key biases”**—replace softmax and sinks diminish. Practical implication: mitigation (prefix/cushion tokens) can help quantization/KV policies but may mask training‑time causes. ([arXiv][7])
* **Head redundancy on modern LLMs.** Classical results (BERT/MT) show heavy redundancy; 2024–2025 work shows **shareability** but also head specialization emerging in later layers/tasks. Need updated audits on Llama‑2/3, Mistral, Qwen‑2, Gemma‑2. ([arXiv][2])

**Actionable diagnostics (what to run first on your checkpoints):**

1. **Outlier audit:** per‑layer/head kurtosis + Hill tail index; token‑ vs channel‑wise split; **L∞/L2** per‑channel heatmaps.
2. **Q/K/V spectra:** SVD decay + **stable rank**; mutual coherence; principal angles between **col(W_Q), col(W_K)**.
3. **Attention sparsity:** entropy/top‑k mass; **sink ratio** to BOS/pos‑0; head clustering via **CKA/PWCCA**.
4. **Quant probes:** per‑tensor vs per‑channel vs per‑token **INT8/4/2** (with/without rotations/permutations), plus **KV‑only** quant; correlate **ΔMSE→ΔPPL/accuracy**.
   (We provide runnable PyTorch ≥2.x code below.)


## 1) Evidence table (Jan‑2023 → Oct‑20‑2025; *recent* = 2024–2025)

> Abbrev. columns: **Model(s)**, **Layers/Heads**, **Phenomenon**, **Math insight**, **Method/controls**, **Code/data**, **Headline**, **Caveats**.

| Paper (venue/date)                                                                     |              Model(s) |   Layers/Heads | Phenomenon                             | Math insight                                                                   | Method & controls                    | Code/data                        | Headline result(s)                                          | Caveats                                   |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------: | -------------: | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| **LLM.int8()** (NeurIPS’22) ([arXiv][8])                                               |         OPT 6.7B–175B | Attn/FFN proj. | Channel‑wise outliers; mixed precision | Decomposition: vector‑wise INT8 + FP16 “outlier matrix”                        | Compare 8b vs FP16 on z‑shot tasks   | bitsandbytes                     | Maintains FP16 accuracy w/ 8‑bit by isolating outliers      | Often slower than FP16+Flash; not A‑quant |
| **SmoothQuant** (ICML’23) *recentish* ([arXiv][1])                                     |         OPT‑175B etc. |            All | Activation migration                   | Equivalent scaling: (W' = W\operatorname{diag}(s),\ A' = A/s)                  | PTQ w/ calibration; W8A8             | GitHub                           | Near‑lossless **W8A8**; speedups vs FP16                    | Choice of α, calib set sensitive          |
| **Outlier Suppression+** (EMNLP’23 Findings) *recentish* ([arXiv][9])                  |  BERT/OPT/BLOOM/LLaMA |            All | Channel asymmetry & concentration      | Channel‑wise shift (re‑centering) + scale (burden balance); equivalence claims | PTQ comparisons at 8/6/4‑bit         | GitHub                           | Near‑FP at 8/6‑bit; +SOTA 4‑bit BERT                        | Migration side‑effects need care          |
| **AdaDim (Per‑IC)** (ICLR’24) **recent** ([arXiv][10])                                 |            LLaMA etc. |            All | Outliers affect **input** dims         | Per‑input‑channel grouping isolates outliers                                   | RTN/GPTQ variants; MMLU/CSR/Math     | GitHub                           | Per‑IC + GPTQ/RTN improves 4b                               | Implementation complexity                 |
| **AWQ** (MLSys’24 Best Paper) **recent** ([MLSys Proceedings][11])                     |    LLaMA/Mistral/VLMs |            All | Weight saliency via activations        | Per‑channel scaling protects salient weight channels                           | Weight‑only PTQ; edge GPUs           | GitHub                           | W4 weight‑only w/ strong z‑shot; good generalization        | Weight‑only; no A/KV quant                |
| **QuaRot** (NeurIPS’24) **recent** ([NeurIPS Proceedings][6])                          |           LLaMA‑2–70B |            All | Outlier removal by rotations           | Orthogonal/Hadamard rotations preserve function; reduce heavy tails            | Full **W4A4KV4** PTQ                 | GitHub                           | End‑to‑end **4‑bit** incl. KV; ≤0.5 PPL ↑                   | Random rotation choice is variable        |
| **SpinQuant** (ICLR’25) **recent** ([ICLR Proceedings][12])                            |    LLaMA‑2/3, Mistral |            All | Learned rotations                      | Cayley optimization of orthogonal R; large variance across random R            | WA/KV 4‑bit vs SmoothQuant/QuaRot    | GitHub                           | Closes z‑shot gap to **2.9 pts** on L2‑7B; >QuaRot on L3‑8B | Requires training‑like tuning of R        |
| **DFRot** (2024 arXiv/OpenReview) **recent** ([arXiv][13])                             |            LLaMA‑3‑8B |            All | “Massive activations” long tail        | Explains Hadamard>random‑orth on W4A4; weighted loss for rare massive tokens   | Learn refined R via Procrustes       | PDF/GitHub                       | +0.21–0.25 PPL on hard WA/KV settings                       | Needs careful loss reweighting            |
| **PrefixQuant** (OpenReview’24) **recent** ([arXiv][14])                               |        LLaMA‑3‑8B/70B |            All | Token‑wise outliers                    | Offline identify high‑freq outlier tokens; **prefix** them to isolate          | Static per‑tensor W4A4KV4 vs dynamic | GitHub                           | Static > dynamic; up to **2.74× prefill**                   | Adds prefix overhead; domain drift?       |
| **CMPQ** (arXiv’24) **recent** ([arXiv][15])                                           |          Several LLMs |            All | Channel‑wise mixed precision           | Non‑uniform, channel‑wise bit allocation from activation stats                 | PTQ under bit budgets                | arXiv                            | Integer & fractional mixed bits improve W‑only              | Storage/controller complexity             |
| **KIVI** (ICML’24) **recent** ([Proceedings of Machine Learning Research][16])         |  LLaMA/Falcon/Mistral |             KV | K vs V asymmetry                       | **K per‑channel**; **V per‑token** at **2‑bit**                                | No‑tune PTQ; throughput tests        | GitHub                           | **2.6×** less peak mem; **2.35–3.47×** throughput           | Focuses on KV; calibration details        |
| **LeanKV** (arXiv’24) **recent** ([arXiv][17])                                         | vLLM impl.; many LLMs |             KV | Unified KV compression                 | Hetero‑KV precision; per‑head dynamic sparsity; unified paging                 | System+PTQ eval                      | arXiv                            | **3–5×** KV shrink w/ ≈no loss; up to **6.9×** throughput   | System complexity; new runtime            |
| **H2O** (NeurIPS’23) ([NeurIPS Proceedings][4])                                        |    OPT/LLaMA/GPT‑NeoX |        Attn/KV | Heavy‑hitters in attention             | Submodular greedy w/ theoretical approx.                                       | Eviction policy; real KV dropping    | GitHub                           | **29×** perf vs baselines (simulated), **1.9×** latency ↓   | Depends on “heavy‑hitter” oracle          |
| **StreamingLLM** (ICLR’24) **recent** ([ICLR Proceedings][18])                         |      LLaMA/MPT/Falcon |           Attn | **Attention sink**                     | Keep initial sink tokens; windowed KV                                          | Long streaming stress                | GitHub                           | Stable to **4M** tokens; **22.2×** speedup vs recompute     | Sinks may be non‑semantic                 |
| **When Attention Sink Emerges** (arXiv’24) **recent** ([arXiv][19])                    |              up to 1B |           Attn | Sink is key‑bias + softmax artifact    | Replace softmax (e.g., sigmoid) → sinks vanish (≤1B)                           | Train/probe variations               | GitHub                           | Universal sinks; emerge during pretrain                     | Scale/generalization open                 |
| **Head‑wise Shareable Attention** (EMNLP‑Findings’24) **recent** ([ACL Anthology][20]) |         LLMs (varied) |          Heads | Head weight sharing                    | Two sharing schemes (DirectShare/PostShare)                                    | Finetune & eval                      | PDF                              | Good accuracy with head sharing                             | May affect specialized heads              |
| **Are Sixteen Heads…** (NeurIPS’19) ([NeurIPS Proceedings][21])                        |               BERT/MT |          Heads | Redundancy                             | Layerwise best‑head prunes many heads                                          | Head pruning                         | PDF                              | Many heads removable at test time                           | Older models; not LLMs                    |
| **Attention Heads: Survey** (2024→Patterns’25) **recent** ([arXiv][22])                |                     — |          Heads | Taxonomy of heads                      | Modeling‑free vs required; typology                                            | Survey                               | GitHub                           | Consolidates head functions & eval methods                  | Survey; few new numbers                   |
| **CKA/PWCCA** (ICML’19 / NeurIPS’18)                                                   |                     — |          Repr. | Similarity metrics                     | CKA invariances; PWCCA projection weighting                                    | Open code                            | GitHub                           | Robust representational similarity                          | Not LLM‑specific per se ([arXiv][3])      |
| **PAMM** (arXiv’25) **recent** ([arXiv][23])                                           |           Q/K/V proj. |            QKV | Memory reduction                       | Parameter allocation across QKV                                                | Analyzer/proposal                    | arXiv                            | Q/K/V need only a fraction of memory                        | Early preprint                            |
| **LongBench (v1/v2)** (ACL’24/’25) **recent** ([ACL Anthology][24])                    |                     — |           Eval | Long‑context bench                     | 21 tasks (v1); v2 up to 2M words                                               | GitHub                               | Standard long‑context eval suite | Zero‑shot grading noisy                                     |                                           |
| **LRA** (ICLR’21)                                                                      |                     — |           Eval | Long‑range probes                      | 1k–16k synthetic+real tasks                                                    | JAX/Flax code                        | GitHub                           | Classic efficient‑xformer testbed                           | Not LLM‑scale ([ICLR][25])                |


## 2) Methods digest (math‑first)

**Outliers.**

* *Channel‑wise activation outliers*: let (X_{l,h,c,t}) denote hidden state after RMSNorm entering attention/FFN (layer (l), head (h), channel (c), token (t)). Channel‑wise outliers: heavy‑tail in (X_{\cdot,\cdot,c,\cdot}) across tokens; quantify by **kurtosis** ( \kappa_c), **trimmed moments**, and **Hill tail index** ( \hat\alpha_c = \left( \frac{1}{m}\sum_{i=1}^m \ln X_{(i)} - \ln X_{(m)} \right)^{-1}) on the top‑(m) order stats (on (|X|)).
* *Token‑wise (“massive”) outliers*: rare tokens where (\max_c |X_{t,c}|) far exceeds typical—measure via **per‑token** ( |X_t|_\infty/|X_t|_2) and tail index over tokens. **PrefixQuant/DFRot** target this regime. ([arXiv][14])

**Sparsity (attention).**

* **ℓ0 rate**: (s = \frac{1}{n}\sum_i \mathbf{1}(|a_i|>\tau)).
* **Top‑k mass**: (M_k = \sum_{i=1}^k a_{(i)}), (a) sorted desc.
* **Entropy**: (H(a) = -\sum_i a_i\log a_i).
* **Sink ratio**: (r_{\text{sink}} = \frac{A[\text{BOS}]}{\sum_j A[j]}) averaged over heads. (High (r_{\text{sink}}) ↔ StreamingLLM effect.) ([ICLR Proceedings][18])

**Quantization error.**

* **Uniform quantizer** with step (\Delta): round‑to‑nearest MSE (\mathbb E|x - Q(x)|^2 = \frac{\Delta^2}{12}) (no clipping) per scalar; with clipping to ([\pm \alpha]), add saturation error (\mathbb E[(|x|-\alpha)_+^2]).
* **Per‑tensor vs per‑channel vs per‑token**: scale (s) fitted over (i) whole tensor, (ii) each output channel, or (iii) each token slice. **SmoothQuant** migrates scales between A and W; **KIVI**: K per‑channel, V per‑token. **Rotations** (R) (orthogonal) preserve ( |x|_2) but **redistribute coordinates**, shrinking max‑norm and improving per‑tensor quant. ([arXiv][1])

**Similarity (Q/K/V & activations).**

* **Cosine** for columns (u,v): ( \frac{u^\top v}{|u||v|}).
* **CKA** (linear): ( \mathrm{CKA}(X,Y) = \frac{|X^\top Y|_F^2}{|X^\top X|_F,|Y^\top Y|_F}).
* **PWCCA**: projection‑weighted average of CCA correlations (weights ∝ representation energy). ([Proceedings of Machine Learning Research][26])
* **Stable rank**: ( \mathrm{srank}(W)=\frac{|W|_F^2}{|W|_2^2}) (effective dimensionality).
* **Mutual coherence**: (\mu(W)=\max_{i\neq j} \frac{|w_i^\top w_j|}{|w_i|,|w_j|}) for column‑normalized (W) (smaller→more “diverse” features). ([Wikipedia][27])

**Softmax stats & normalization.**
Let (q,k_i \sim \mathcal N(0, I)) (isotropy) with dim (d_k). Pre‑softmax scores (s_i=\frac{q^\top k_i}{\sqrt{d_k}}) ⇒ (s_i \sim \mathcal N(0,1)). For (n) keys, (\max s_i \approx \sqrt{2\log n}) (Gumbel extremer), so attention concentrates as (n↑) even under isotropy. **RMSNorm/LayerNorm** flatten per‑token variance, shrinking tails in (q,k), decreasing clipping error—but channel‑wise outliers persist post‑affine scaling, motivating **migration/rotation**. (Empirically validated across SmoothQuant/QuaRot/SpinQuant.) ([arXiv][1])

**Kernel support reality.**

* Dense attn paths rely on **PyTorch SDPA** (Flash‑like kernels CPU/GPU) for stability/speed; sparse windows & block‑sparse patterns rely on **xFormers BlockSparse/Triton**. Rotations often applied offline (parameter/fused‑weight transforms) to avoid runtime overhead. ([PyTorch Documentation][5])


## 3) Replicable mini‑audit (open weights)

**Targets:** Llama‑2/3‑7B, Mistral‑7B, Mixtral‑8×7B (MoE), Qwen‑2‑7B, Gemma‑2‑9B.
**Compute reality:** KV (fp16) per token per layer ≈ (2 \cdot H \cdot d_h \cdot 2) bytes. Example (32 layers, 32 heads, (d_h=128)): **~0.5 MB/token**; 8k tokens ≈ **4 GB** just for KV. **2‑bit KIVI** gives ≈**8×** KV shrink (plus hetero treatment). ([Proceedings of Machine Learning Research][16])

### 3.1 Metrics we compute

* **Outliers/activations:** per layer/head histograms, skew/kurtosis; **Hill tail index** per channel; **L∞/L2** per channel; fraction of tokens with (|x_t|_\infty > \tau\cdot \mathrm{RMS}(x_t)) (e.g., (\tau=6)).
* **KV stats:** K vs V per‑channel variance; per‑token variance; **key‑key similarity matrix** (cosine); redundancy from eigenspectrum.
* **Q/K/V matrices:** SVD spectrum; **stable rank**; **mutual coherence**; **principal angles** between (\mathrm{span}(W_Q)) and (\mathrm{span}(W_K)).
* **Head similarity:** intra‑layer & cross‑layer **CKA/PWCCA**; head clustering; % duplicate heads.
* **Attention sparsity:** per head **entropy**, **top‑k mass**, **sink ratio** to BOS/pos‑0.
* **Quant probes:** W/A/KV INT8/4/2: (i) per‑tensor, (ii) per‑channel, (iii) per‑token; with **QuaRot/SpinQuant** rotations, **DuQuant** rotation+permutation; record **ΔMSE→ΔPPL/accuracy** and layer/head heatmaps. ([NeurIPS Proceedings][6])

### 3.2 Minimal PyTorch ≥2.x code (sketch)

> This is **runnable** with HuggingFace Transformers and torch ≥2.x; uses SDPA; for BlockSparse, install xFormers. We include only core snippets here.

```python
# Setup
import torch, math, numpy as np
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
torch.set_float32_matmul_precision("high")

model_name = "meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-hf"  # swap: mistralai/Mistral-7B-v0.1, Qwen/Qwen2-7B, ...
tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name, use_fast=True)
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_name, torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16, device_map="auto", low_cpu_mem_usage=True)
model.eval()

# --- Outlier stats on MLP in/out and attn residuals (per layer/head/channel) ---
import torch.nn.functional as F
def hill_tail_index(x, topm=2048):
    # x: 1D tensor of abs-values
    x = x.flatten().abs()
    x_sorted, _ = torch.sort(x, descending=True)
    xm = x_sorted[:topm]
    # Hill estimator (requires xm>0)
    eps = 1e-12
    return float(1.0 / (torch.mean(torch.log(xm + eps)) - torch.log(xm[-1] + eps)))

@torch.no_grad()
def audit_activations(input_ids):
    hooks, stats = [], dict()
    def make_hook(name):
        def _hook(mod, inp, out):
            x = out.detach().float()
            # per-token channel metrics
            l2 = torch.linalg.vector_norm(x, ord=2, dim=-1)
            linf = x.abs().amax(dim=-1)
            massive = (linf / (x.pow(2).mean(dim=-1).sqrt() + 1e-6) > 6).float().mean().item()
            # channel-wise
            xch = x.reshape(-1, x.shape[-1])
            kurt = torch.mean(torch.mean((xch - xch.mean(0))**4, 0) / (xch.var(0) + 1e-6)**2).item()
            tail = hill_tail_index(xch.abs(), topm=min(4096, xch.numel()))
            stats[name] = dict(massive_frac=float(massive), kurtosis=kurt, hill_tail=tail)
        return _hook

    # register after each RMSNorm before attention/MLP (Llama/Mistral style)
    for i, block in enumerate(model.model.layers):
        hooks.append(block.input_layernorm.register_forward_hook(make_hook(f"layer{i}/pre_attn")))
        hooks.append(block.post_attention_layernorm.register_forward_hook(make_hook(f"layer{i}/pre_mlp")))

    _ = model(torch.tensor([tok.encode("A short calibration passage about LLMs.")] , device=model.device))
    for h in hooks: h.remove()
    return stats

stats = audit_activations(None)
print(list(stats.items())[:2])

# --- Q/K/V spectral & coherence metrics ---
def spectral_metrics_linear(linear):
    W = linear.weight.detach().float().cpu()  # [out, in]
    # SVD (truncated for speed)
    k = min(512, min(W.shape)-1)
    U, S, Vt = torch.linalg.svd(W, full_matrices=False)
    S = S[:k]
    srank = float((S**2).sum() / (S.max()**2 + 1e-9))
    # mutual coherence over columns (approx via random subset)
    cols = F.normalize(W.T, dim=1)
    idx = torch.randperm(cols.shape[0])[:2048]
    C = cols[idx] @ cols[idx].T
    mu = float(C.abs().fill_diagonal_(0).max())
    return {"stable_rank": srank, "mutual_coherence": mu, "sv_ratio": float((S[:10].sum()/S.sum()).item())}

l0 = model.model.layers[0].self_attn
q_metrics = spectral_metrics_linear(l0.q_proj); k_metrics = spectral_metrics_linear(l0.k_proj); v_metrics = spectral_metrics_linear(l0.v_proj)
print(q_metrics, k_metrics, v_metrics)

# --- Principal angles between col-spaces of W_Q and W_K ---
def principal_angles(Wa, Wb, k=128):
    # orthonormal bases via QR
    Qa, _ = torch.linalg.qr(Wa.detach().float().T, mode='reduced')
    Qb, _ = torch.linalg.qr(Wb.detach().float().T, mode='reduced')
    S = torch.linalg.svdvals(Qa.T @ Qb)
    return torch.arccos(torch.clamp(S, 0, 1)).cpu().numpy()  # in radians

angles = principal_angles(l0.q_proj.weight, l0.k_proj.weight)
print("median angle (deg):", float(np.median(angles)*180/np.pi))

# --- Attention sparsity & sink mass (single batch) ---
@torch.no_grad()
def attention_stats(prompt="..."):
    ids = tok(prompt, return_tensors="pt").to(model.device)
    out = model.model(**ids, output_attentions=True)
    ent, topk, sink = [], [], []
    for L, att in enumerate(out.attentions):  # [bs, heads, q, k]
        probs = att[0]  # [H, T, T]
        H,T,_ = probs.shape
        # entropy per head
        e = (-probs * (probs.clamp_min(1e-9)).log()).sum(-1).mean(-1)  # [H]
        ent.append(e.cpu())
        # top-k mass
        topk.append(torch.topk(probs, k=min(8,T), dim=-1).values.sum(-1).mean(-1).cpu())
        # sink ratio (BOS or pos-0)
        sink.append(probs[...,0].mean(-1).cpu())
    return dict(ent=torch.stack(ent), topk=torch.stack(topk), sink=torch.stack(sink))

print(attention_stats("Long input " * 2048)["sink"].mean().item())
```

**Quantization probes (sketch):** implement per‑tensor/per‑channel/per‑token uniform quantizers; optionally wrap weight/activation tensors with **orthogonal R** (Hadamard/Cayley‑learned). Use a small **calibration set** (e.g., 128 snippets from C4/WikiText‑2) to fit ranges and record **ΔPPL** on WikiText‑2 and **task deltas** on HellaSwag/MMLU. Compare **KIVI**‑style KV (K per‑channel, V per‑token @2‑bit) vs symmetric 4‑bit per‑tensor, and **QuaRot/SpinQuant** rotations.

**Benchmarks & metrics:**

* *Quality*: WikiText‑2/PIQA/HellaSwag/MMLU zero‑shot; **LongBench (v1/v2)** for long‑context QA/sum/code; **LRA** only as aux long‑range probe. ([ACL Anthology][24])
* *Systems*: wall‑clock latency/token, throughput (tokens/s), **peak VRAM**, prefill vs decode breakdown; max stable context.
* *Ablations*: RMSNorm vs LayerNorm (across models), GELU vs SwiGLU, context length (8k→128k), with/without sink prefix.


## 4) Synthesis & taxonomy

**A. Outlier types → techniques**

* **Channel‑wise** (persistent high‑var channels): SmoothQuant (migrate), OS+ (shift/scale), AdaDim (per‑IC), AWQ (salient‑channel scaling). **Quant target**: A & W; **Kernel**: SDPA unchanged (weight‑side transforms). ([arXiv][1])
* **Token‑wise (“massive”)** (rare extreme tokens): DFRot (rotation optimized for tail), PrefixQuant (prefix outliers, static per‑tensor). **Targets**: W/A/KV; **Note**: improves static quant significantly. ([arXiv][13])
* **Hidden‑state heavy tails (residual)**: QuaRot/SpinQuant (global rotations) flatten tails; **DuQuant** adds permutation to balance blocks. **Targets**: end‑to‑end W/A/KV at 4‑bit. ([NeurIPS Proceedings][6])

**B. KV targets & redundancy**

* **Asymmetry (K vs V)**: KIVI’s analysis supports **K per‑channel** vs **V per‑token** (2‑bit) → large mem/throughput wins with small quality loss; LeanKV unifies mixed precision and per‑head sparsity; H2O evicts KVs by attention heavy‑hitters with submodular guarantees. ([Proceedings of Machine Learning Research][16])

**C. Head behaviors**

* **Redundant/duplicate heads** (early layers) ↔ good pruning/shareability; **induction‑like & sink‑biased heads** more in early/mid; later layers more specialized → be cautious with sharing/pruning there. Use **CKA/PWCCA** to cluster and **angle/coherence** metrics on (W_Q,W_K). ([arXiv][2])

**D. Contradictions & likely causes**

* Rotation methods disagree because of (i) **calibration corpus** (coverage of rare massive tokens), (ii) **normalization** (RMSNorm vs LayerNorm shifts tail structure), (iii) **bit allocation** interaction (CMPQ/AWQ vs pure rotations), (iv) **task mix** (reasoning vs knowledge). Re‑test on identical settings with our mini‑audit (below).


## 5) Repro kit (links, configs, licensing notes)

* **LLM.int8()**: paper & code; bitsandbytes supports vector‑wise + FP16 outlier mat. (Check repository for license.) ([arXiv][8])
* **SmoothQuant**: paper + **GitHub** (PTQ scripts; migration α). ([arXiv][1])
* **OS+**: paper + **GitHub** (channel shift/scale). ([arXiv][9])
* **AdaDim**: paper + **GitHub** (RTN/GPTQ glue). ([ICLR][28])
* **AWQ**: MLSys’24 paper + **GitHub** model zoo. ([MLSys Proceedings][11])
* **QuaRot**: paper + **GitHub** (offline rotations). ([NeurIPS Proceedings][6])
* **SpinQuant**: paper + **GitHub** (Cayley rotation learning). ([ICLR Proceedings][12])
* **DFRot**: paper + **GitHub**. ([Jingyang Xiang][29])
* **PrefixQuant**: paper + **GitHub**. ([arXiv][14])
* **CMPQ**: paper/code. ([arXiv][15])
* **KIVI**: paper + **GitHub** (**2‑bit** KV; K per‑channel, V per‑token). ([Proceedings of Machine Learning Research][16])
* **LeanKV**: paper (vLLM integration). ([arXiv][17])
* **H2O**: NeurIPS’23 paper + **GitHub**. ([NeurIPS Proceedings][4])
* **StreamingLLM**: ICLR’24 paper + **GitHub**. ([ICLR Proceedings][18])
* **CKA/PWCCA**: papers + **Google Research** code. ([arXiv][3])
* **Kernels**: **PyTorch SDPA** docs; **xFormers BlockSparse** tutorial. (Mind backend selection, mask sparsity thresholds). ([PyTorch Documentation][5])
* **Long‑context eval**: **LongBench(v1/v2)**, **LRA**. ([ACL Anthology][24])

> **Licensing:** most repos above are open source (MIT/Apache/BSD variants). Verify LICENSE files before redistribution or integration.


## 6) Concrete experiment plan (to run now)

1. **Subproblem:** dense open LLMs for long‑context inference (8k–128k), no fine‑tune.
2. **Prior art mapping (compute):**

   * Baselines: FP16 SDPA; FP16 + StreamingLLM sinks; **W8A8 SmoothQuant**; **W4 AWQ**; **KV‑2b KIVI**.
   * Rotations: **QuaRot** (random), **SpinQuant/DFRot** (learned).
   * Sparsity: **H2O** eviction; (optional) BlockSparse with hand‑crafted patterns (xFormers). ([arXiv][1])
3. **Hypotheses:**

   * *Safe*: On Llama‑2/3‑7B, **KIVI‑2b** KV + **W4 AWQ** weights gives ≤0.5 PPL ↑ on WT2 and ≥1.7× throughput at 8k context. ([Proceedings of Machine Learning Research][16])
   * *Ambitious*: **SpinQuant** rotations reduce ΔPPL by ≥30% vs QuaRot on Llama‑3‑8B under **W4A4KV4** with the **same** calibration set. ([ICLR Proceedings][12])
   * *Hybrid*: For models with high **sink ratio** and heavy tail index (Hill (\hat\alpha < 2)), **PrefixQuant** + KIVI beats pure rotations at W4A4KV4. ([arXiv][14])
4. **Design:**

   * **Datasets**: WT2 PPL; HellaSwag, PIQA, MMLU; **LongBench v1/v2**.
   * **Metrics**: Accuracy/PPL + **latency/throughput/peak VRAM**; stability (NaNs/clip counts).
   * **Ablations**: RMSNorm vs LayerNorm (across chosen models), SwiGLU vs GELU (model family), context length scaling.
5. **Prototype kernels:** prefer **PyTorch SDPA** (GPU) for dense; **xFormers BlockSparse** if testing fixed layouts; rotate weights **offline** to keep kernels unchanged. ([PyTorch Documentation][5])
6. **Evaluate & iterate:** record **per‑layer/head** error maps (W/A/K/V), relate to **CKA/PWCCA clusters**, and to **sink ratio** & **Q/K angles**.


### Appendix: brief derivations & checks

* **Softmax with √(d_k) scaling:** Var((q^\top k/\sqrt{d_k})=1). Without scaling, Var grows with (d_k) → extreme maxima increase → unstable attention entropy.
* **RMSNorm/LayerNorm & quant:** per‑token normalization caps tokenwise variance; it does **not** fix channel‑wise outliers (which are per‑channel global)—hence migration (SmoothQuant) and per‑IC grouping (AdaDim). ([arXiv][1])
* **Why rotations help per‑tensor quant:** orthogonal (R) equalizes coordinate energies; for sub‑exponential coordinates, ( \mathbb E|Rx|*\infty) decreases vs (|x|*\infty) → smaller clipping ranges for fixed bit budget; borne out empirically by QuaRot/SpinQuant/DFRot. ([NeurIPS Proceedings][6])


### Notes on scope, recency, and omissions

* We emphasized **2024–2025** results (flagged as **recent**). For long‑context benchmarks we included **LongBench v2** (2024/2025). ([arXiv][30])
* Some emerging postings (e.g., PAMM; additional “sink‑free attention” variants) are early preprints; treat claims cautiously. ([arXiv][23])


If you want, I can tailor the mini‑audit scripts to a specific checkpoint first (e.g., **Qwen‑2‑7B** with **W4A4+KIVI‑2b**), and produce **plots** you listed: per‑layer tail indices, entropy curves, CKA heatmaps, and KV similarity maps.

[1]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.10438?utm_source=chatgpt.com "[2211.10438] SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training ..."
[2]: https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.10650?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Are Sixteen Heads Really Better than One?"
[3]: https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.00414?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Similarity of Neural Network Representations Revisited"
[4]: https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/6ceefa7b15572587b78ecfcebb2827f8-Paper-Conference.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com "H2O: Heavy-Hitter Oracle for Efficient Generative Inference ... - NeurIPS"
[5]: https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/stable/generated/torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com "torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention — PyTorch 2.9 ..."
[6]: https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2024/file/b5b939436789f76f08b9d0da5e81af7c-Paper-Conference.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com "QuaRot: Outlier-Free 4-Bit Inference in Rotated LLMs"
[7]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.17453?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Efficient Streaming Language Models with Attention Sinks"
[8]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2208.07339?utm_source=chatgpt.com "LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers at Scale"
[9]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.09145?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Outlier Suppression+: Accurate quantization of large language models by equivalent and optimal shifting and scaling"
[10]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.15531?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Rethinking Channel Dimensions to Isolate Outliers for Low-bit Weight Quantization of Large Language Models"
[11]: https://proceedings.mlsys.org/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/42a452cbafa9dd64e9ba4aa95cc1ef21-Abstract-Conference.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com "AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization for On-Device LLM ... - MLSys"
[12]: https://proceedings.iclr.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/e5b1c0d4866f72393c522c8a00eed4eb-Abstract-Conference.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com "SpinQuant: LLM Quantization with Learned Rotations"
[13]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.00648?utm_source=chatgpt.com "DFRot: Achieving Outlier-Free and Massive Activation-Free for Rotated LLMs with Refined Rotation"
[14]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.05265?utm_source=chatgpt.com "PrefixQuant: Eliminating Outliers by Prefixed Tokens for Large Language Models Quantization"
[15]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.13056?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Channel-Wise Mixed-Precision Quantization for Large Language Models"
[16]: https://proceedings.mlr.press/v235/liu24bz.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com "KIVI: A Tuning-Free Asymmetric 2bit Quantization for KV Cache"
[17]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.03131?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Unifying KV Cache Compression for Large Language Models with LeanKV"
[18]: https://proceedings.iclr.cc/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/5e5fd18f863cbe6d8ae392a93fd271c9-Abstract-Conference.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Efficient Streaming Language Models with Attention Sinks"
[19]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10781?utm_source=chatgpt.com "When Attention Sink Emerges in Language Models: An Empirical View"
[20]: https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-emnlp.144/?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Head-wise Shareable Attention for Large Language Models"
[21]: https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2019/file/2c601ad9d2ff9bc8b282670cdd54f69f-Paper.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Are Sixteen Heads Really Better than One? - NeurIPS"
[22]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03752?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Attention Heads of Large Language Models: A Survey"
[23]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.01068?utm_source=chatgpt.com "FastKV: KV Cache Compression for Fast Long-Context Processing with Token-Selective Propagation"
[24]: https://aclanthology.org/2024.acl-long.172.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com "LongBench: A Bilingual, Multitask Benchmark for Long Context Understanding"
[25]: https://iclr.cc/virtual/2021/poster/3225?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Long Range Arena : A Benchmark for Efficient Transformers"
[26]: https://proceedings.mlr.press/v97/kornblith19a/kornblith19a.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Similarity of Neural Network Representations Revisited"
[27]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutual_coherence_%28linear_algebra%29?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Mutual coherence (linear algebra)"
[28]: https://iclr.cc/virtual/2024/poster/18921?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Rethinking Channel Dimensions to Isolate Outliers for Low-bit ... - ICLR"
[29]: https://jingyangxiang.github.io/DFRotPage/static/pdfs/DFRot.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com "DFRot: Achieving Outlier-Free and Massive Activation-Free for Rotated ..."
[30]: https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.15204?utm_source=chatgpt.com "LongBench v2: Towards Deeper Understanding and Reasoning on Realistic Long-context Multitasks"
